meta.title: Air Decompression Table
meta.source: US Navy Diving Manual Rev 7 Change A (2018)
meta.coverage_notes: USN Rev 7 Table 9-9 AIR decompression schedules for the standard depth ladder 30-190 fsw (every 5 ft to 60, then every 10 ft to 190). AIR schedule ONLY: the manual prints two rows per bottom-time (AIR and AIR/O2); the AIR/O2 in-water-oxygen decompression row is intentionally dropped because this tool models single-gas air with no gas switching, and the repetitive (ending) group is shared across both rows, so nothing is lost for the 9-8 chain. AIR/O2 in-water-O2 schedules do exist in the source table but are out of scope and not modeled here. Only bottom-times that carry a Navy-assigned ending repetitive group are included; longer bottom-times whose Repet Group column is blank in the manual are omitted (they truncate the tail of each block and yield a clean TableRangeError rather than a fabricated group). Shallowest decompression stop is 20 fsw (Rev 7 uses no 10-fsw air stop). total_stop_min is the SUM of the AIR stop-column minutes (NOT the 'Total Ascent Time', which includes travel/ascent). time_to_first_stop_min is the manual's M:S value converted to decimal minutes. exceptional_exposure=true for rows below the boxed 'Exceptional Exposure' dividers. Transcribed by reading the official Rev 7 tables PDF page images (docs/tables/_render/page-06..24) cell-by-cell and mapping every stop cell to its stop-depth column, cross-checked two ways on every row: (1) time_to_first_stop*30 == depth - deepest_stop_depth, and (2) total_stop_min == TotalAscentTime - time_to_first_stop - 0.6667 (the fixed 20-fsw-to-surface leg). Depths 30-150 were additionally re-verified against the images and left unchanged (their auto-scraped values matched the PDF exactly). Depths 200-300 (exceptional exposure) and the AIR/O2 schedules are out of scope.
meta.unverified_warning: Transcribed from the official US Navy Rev 7 tables PDF (2026-07-04); spot-check against your manual before real use.
